burial.plot_notes: West Cemetery, Madison (formerly East Guilford), New Haven, Connecticut

parents.father_research_notes: Thomas Norton of Guilford (b. Mar 4, 1675; d. Sep 21, 1740) — miller and wheelwright

burial.plot_notes: West Cemetery, Madison (formerly East Guilford), New Haven, Connecticut

parents.father_research_notes: Thomas Norton of Guilford (b. Mar 4, 1675; d. Sep 21, 1740) — miller and wheelwright

sources.full: Israel P. Warren D.D., The Stanley Families of America (1887)

narrative_blocks.header: Lived nearly fifty years in Madison Connecticut
narrative_blocks.body: Arah Norton was born in Guilford CT in 1739 and married Phebe Scranton of East Guilford in 1764. They settled at Madison — the village that East Guilford had become — and stayed there. Arah died in Madison in 1813 at seventy-four; Phebe followed five years later.

parents.father_research_notes: Josiah Scranton of East Guilford (now Madison) CT. Per EH 1909 entry 398; not built per Jalapeño.
parents.mother_research_notes: Mary Dickenson Scranton of East Guilford CT. Per EH 1909 entry 398; not built per Jalapeño.

narrative_blocks.header: Eldest daughter omitted from EH's record
narrative_blocks.body: Rhoda Norton was born in 1763 — three years before her brother Josiah, the eldest child whom Edward Hooker named in his 1909 genealogy. WikiTree documents Rhoda's marriage to Zebul Norton in Waterbury in 1782 and four children. The reason for her omission from EH is not established; she may have been overlooked as a daughter who married out of Connecticut documentation.

narrative_blocks.header: Returned to Guilford in widowhood
narrative_blocks.body: Hannah Norton Hotchkiss married Amos Hotchkiss in East Guilford on December 12, 1791, and they raised their family in Wallingford CT. Amos died in 1808 at forty-three; Hannah lived another forty-three years and died in Guilford — her natal town — in 1851.

narrative_blocks.header: Two daughters married the same Dowd
narrative_blocks.body: Mabel Norton's daughters Adelia and Phebe both married Harvey H. Dowd — a sororate marriage in which Phebe married her sister's widower after Adelia's death. The pattern was common in nineteenth-century New England when widowers needed help with young children.

career.notes: Self-made farmer. Cleared land and built a log cabin upon arrival in Addison County VT about 1800. Owned 500 acres at the time of his death in 1833. The homestead was inherited by his son Hiram Norton.

narrative_blocks.header: Cleared Vermont homestead at age twenty-three
narrative_blocks.body: Abel Norton was born in Connecticut in 1777 and migrated to Addison County, Vermont about 1800. He cleared land, built a log cabin, and farmed there the rest of his life. By his 1833 death he owned five hundred acres and was called in the local history 'a self-made man.' His son Hiram inherited the homestead.

narrative_blocks.header: Raised ten children eight survived to adulthood
narrative_blocks.body: Abel and Mehitable raised ten children at the Vermont homestead. Per the 19th-century Addison County local history, eight of the ten survived to adulthood — a high rate for the early-1800s frontier. Two children are presently identified: Calvin and Hiram.

narrative_blocks.header: Died at fifty-one in Guilford
narrative_blocks.body: Diadema Norton Murray died July 10, 1837 in Guilford CT at age fifty-one — twenty-seven years after the death of her husband Calvin Murray. She had been widowed at twenty-five with three young children all under five, of whom her youngest Beulah Maria was born just six weeks before Calvin's 1810 death.

bio.notes: Also spelled "Diademia" in some sources. Married Dudley.

narrative_blocks.header: Married a widower with eight years between
narrative_blocks.body: Diadema Norton was twenty-eight when she married Joseph Dudley as his second wife. Joseph was thirty-four — six years her senior — and had been previously married, though his first wife is not named in EH. They had one documented child, Darlington, who later removed to Vermont.

narrative_blocks.header: Remarried in Vermont at age seventy-three
narrative_blocks.body: Diadema Norton Dudley was widowed in 1805 when her first husband Joseph Dudley died. Eleven years later, at age seventy-three, she migrated to Vermont and married Phineas Spaulding in Panton, Addison County on August 13, 1816. Phineas was sixty-seven and twenty years her junior.

parents.father_research_notes: Joseph Dudley Sr. of East Guilford CT. Per EH 1909 entry 400.
parents.mother_research_notes: Silence Dowd Dudley of East Guilford CT. Per EH 1909 entry 400.

bio.extra_names: EH 1909 records first name as 'Darlington'; FamilySearch records 'Darling' (LH1D-CD7) — FS preferred per project rule

narrative_blocks.header: Married at twenty-two had four children
narrative_blocks.body: Darling Dudley — recorded as 'Darlington' by Edward Hooker in 1909 but as 'Darling' by FamilySearch — married Artemisia Stannard on February 1, 1796 in Guilford CT at age twenty-two. They had four children spanning twenty years: Fannie (1796), Mary Ann (1798), Horace Sherman (1803), and Charles M. (1816). EH records his removal to Vermont, but the FamilySearch-documented Connecticut births of all four children show this migration was late.

bio.notes: Replacement naming after first Hooker Norton (H00399) died Sep 29, 1742
bio.extra_names: Called 'No. 2' in EH 1909 because his elder brother Hooker Norton (H00399, b. 1741) had received the same name — the elder Hooker presumably died young and the parents reused the name in 1744.

narrative_blocks.header: Given his deceased elder brother's name
narrative_blocks.body: Hooker Norton was the SECOND son of Reuben and Hannah Hooker Norton to be given the name Hooker. The first (, b. 1741) presumably died young. Three years later when this son was born in 1744 the parents reused the Hooker name — a common eighteenth-century replacement-naming pattern that EH itself notes by labeling him 'No. 2.'

narrative_blocks.header: Eleven children five died young
narrative_blocks.body: Hooker and Sibyl Bradley Norton had eleven documented children. Five died in infancy or childhood: Daniel Hooker at nine months in 1764; Russell at age six in 1774; Hannah at age eight in 1787; a second Russell at age eleven in 1795; and Zenas at age nine that same year. The 1795 deaths of Russell and Zenas in the same year hint at an epidemic.

narrative_blocks.header: Lived eighty-three years through three wars
narrative_blocks.body: Hooker Norton was born in colonial Connecticut on January 15, 1744 — age eleven at the start of the French and Indian War, age thirty-two when Independence was declared. He outlived the Revolutionary War, the War of 1812, and his wife Sibyl by twenty-one years. He died July 17, 1827 in Madison CT at eighty-three.

parents.father_research_notes: Joseph Bradley of Vergennes VT. Per EH 1909 entry 401.
parents.mother_research_notes: Sibyl Meigs Bradley of Vergennes VT. Per EH 1909 entry 401.

narrative_blocks.header: Family migrated to Vermont before her death
narrative_blocks.body: Sibyl Bradley Norton was born in Guilford CT in 1745 and married Hooker Norton there in 1762 at seventeen. By her 1806 death the family had migrated to Sunderland, Bennington County, Vermont — establishing that the Hooker Norton household made the New England westward move sometime between 1786 (when her youngest son Zenas was born in CT) and 1806. Her husband Hooker returned to Madison CT to die in 1827.

narrative_blocks.header: Two sisters married Evarts brothers
narrative_blocks.body: Lorain Norton married Ezra Evarts of Guilford in 1783; nine years later her younger sister Lois married Ezra's brother Curtis. Both Evarts brothers were sons of Jonathan and Lucretia Parmalee Evarts. Lorain and Ezra removed to Vermont; Lois and Curtis stayed in Connecticut.

narrative_blocks.header: Three Evarts siblings married three Nortons
narrative_blocks.body: Lorain Norton married Ezra Evarts in 1783. Years later Ezra's brother Curtis married Lorain's sister Lois, and their sister Mabel Evarts married Lorain's uncle Eber Norton. Three Evarts siblings married three Nortons across two generations — an unusually dense intermarriage between the two Guilford families.

narrative_blocks.header: Three Evarts siblings married three Nortons
narrative_blocks.body: Lois Norton married Curtis Evarts in 1792. Her sister Lorain had married Curtis's brother Ezra nine years before, and the Evartses' sister Mabel married Lois's uncle Eber Norton. Three Evarts siblings tied themselves to three Nortons — Guilford genealogy at its densest.

burial.plot_notes: Hindes Cemetery, Addison, Addison County VT. FindAGrave indicates burial here despite EH-recorded death in Mexico NY 1845 — most likely his remains were returned to Addison alongside his first wife Lydia.

narrative_blocks.header: Wife died three days after twins born
narrative_blocks.body: Bela Norton's first wife Lydia Bushnell gave birth to unnamed twins on March 17, 1813. She died three days later on March 20 — strongly suggesting complications from the birth. Bela went on to marry Mary Paine and then Mary Ann Mulliken.

narrative_blocks.header: Migrated west to Mexico New York
narrative_blocks.body: Bela Norton was born in East Guilford CT in 1772 but ended his life two thousand miles away — in Mexico, Oswego County, New York, where he died in June 1845 at age seventy-two. The migration date is not documented; he had spent his early married years in Connecticut.

narrative_blocks.header: Migrated four states across his lifetime
narrative_blocks.body: Bela Norton's life traced the New England westward migration in four stages: born in Connecticut in 1772; married Lydia Bushnell in Cheshire, Massachusetts in 1794; raised his children in Addison, Vermont where Lydia died 1813; ultimately died in Mexico, Oswego County, New York in 1845. His remains appear to have been returned to Vermont — buried Hindes Cemetery, Addison.

narrative_blocks.header: Two brothers married two Hill sisters
narrative_blocks.body: Benjamin Norton married Damaris Hill of East Guilford in the 1790s. Years later his younger brother Daniel married Damaris's sister Lois Hill. The two Norton brothers raised their twenty-one combined children with two Hill sisters as wives — a sibling-double marriage that doubled the cousin relationships in the next generation.

narrative_blocks.header: Lost two teenage daughters in a year
narrative_blocks.body: Daniel Norton and Lois Hill Norton lost their daughters Catherine (age fifteen) and Eliza (age thirteen) within nine months of each other in 1835-1836. Catherine died April 1835; Eliza followed January 1836. The cause is not documented but the close timing suggests an epidemic illness.

burial.plot_notes: West Cemetery, Madison (formerly East Guilford), New Haven County CT. FindAGrave.

narrative_blocks.header: Died at thirty-eight leaving six children
narrative_blocks.body: Hannah Norton Allis died March 12, 1785 at age thirty-eight in East Guilford, leaving Nathaniel a widower with six children. The youngest, Pierce, was only sixteen months old. Nathaniel never remarried and lived another forty years.

burial.plot_notes: West Cemetery, Madison New Haven County CT. FindAGrave.

parents.father_research_notes: Nathaniel Allis Sr. of East Guilford CT. Per EH 1909 entry 402.
parents.mother_research_notes: Hannah Scranton Allis of East Guilford CT. Per EH 1909 entry 402.

military_service.notes: Served as a private in the American Revolutionary War per FindAGrave.

narrative_blocks.header: Forty years a widower with six children
narrative_blocks.body: Nathaniel Allis was forty-four when his wife Hannah died in 1785, leaving him with six children including a sixteen-month-old. He never remarried and lived another forty years to age eighty-three. He had served as a private in the American Revolutionary War a decade before Hannah's death.

burial.plot_notes: Ely Cemetery, Lyme New London County CT. FindAGrave.

narrative_blocks.header: Married at thirty named son for drowned uncle
narrative_blocks.body: Rebecca Allis was thirty years old when she married Thomas Anderson in 1810 — late by the standards of her era. They named their only documented son Samuel Pierce Anderson, carrying the name of Rebecca's youngest brother Pierce who had drowned in 1803 at nineteen.

narrative_blocks.header: Drowned in 1803 at age nineteen
narrative_blocks.body: Pierce Allis was the youngest of six children and only sixteen months old when his mother Hannah died in 1785. He drowned March 20, 1803 at nineteen. Edward Hooker recorded the drowning but not the location or circumstance — likely the Connecticut Sound where the family had its East Guilford homestead.

narrative_blocks.header: Married at twenty-two had thirteen children
narrative_blocks.body: Reuben Norton Jr. married Lois Cruttenden of East Guilford about 1770 — when he was twenty-two and she was twelve. The age gap implies the marriage date is an approximation; the real wedding likely came years later. They had at least thirteen children documented across EH and FamilySearch.

bio.extra_names: EH 1909 spells maiden surname 'Crittenden'; FamilySearch gives 'Cruttenden' (KZSD-2DZ) — FS preferred

narrative_blocks.header: Married a widower in her late thirties
narrative_blocks.body: Hannah Norton was approximately thirty-eight when she married Martin Blatchley as his second wife on July 8, 1813. They had three children, eldest born 1816. She outlived Martin by six months — he died November 3, 1847 and she followed May 14, 1848.

narrative_blocks.header: Died at thirty-seven leaving three children
narrative_blocks.body: Hulbert Norton died March 11, 1823 in Guilford at thirty-seven, leaving Laura Dudley with three children — the youngest, Maria, was not yet two. Laura never remarried and lived another thirty-eight years to 1861.

narrative_blocks.header: Outlived husband by forty-seven years
narrative_blocks.body: Leah Norton was forty-seven when her husband Alexander McQuillan died November 4, 1839 in Guilford. She never remarried and lived another forty-seven years, dying at Guilford January 19, 1886 at age ninety-three.

narrative_blocks.header: Fathered fourteen children with two wives
narrative_blocks.body: Hebron Norton had fourteen children across two marriages — first to Susan Truman, then to a woman named only Julia in Edward Hooker's record. None of the fourteen are individually named in the 1909 source. FamilySearch's implausible 1900 death date would put him at 105-106 and is likely an error.

death.notes: Estimated ~60yr lifespan; m. Jesse Murray (d.1824), no death record

military_service.notes: DAR Patriot Ancestor A006980; NSSAR Ancestor #110056.

burial.plot_notes: Town Line Cemetery, Bridport, Addison County VT (per Findagrave). Same cemetery as her father Deacon Ichabod Bartlett Jr (X00205) and her sister Marina Bartlett Wright (HD3189).
Town Line Cemetery, Bridport, Addison County VT (per Findagrave). Same cemetery as her father Deacon Ichabod Bartlett Jr.

narrative_blocks.header: Lived to 103, outlived husband 35 years
narrative_blocks.body: Marina Bartlett Wright lived to age ~103, dying in Addison VT in March 1887 — long enough to span the entire 19th century from Jefferson's first term to Cleveland's. She married John Wright at age 53 in 1837 and they had no documented children; she outlived him by 35 years after his 1852 death.

burial.plot_notes: Brick Church Cemetery, Greenbush, Sheboygan County WI, Row 7 (per Findagrave).

military_service.unit: Capt Robbins's Company, Col. William Williams's 4th Regiment, Vermont detached militia (1812)
military_service.notes: Served as Private during the War of 1812 under Capt Robbins, in Col. William Williams's 4th Regiment of Vermont detached militia. Wife Hannah filed widow's pension after his death; Sam's source notes the citation could not be located.

bio.notes: Named for ancestor Thomas Hooker — through maternal grandmother Azubah Norton, granddaughter of Hannah Hooker
Surname "Standley/Stanley" used as given name — honors maternal grandmother Sarah Standley Hooker

burial.plot_notes: West Cemetery, Madison New Haven County CT. FindAGrave.

narrative_blocks.header: Three Evarts siblings married three Nortons
narrative_blocks.body: Eber Norton married Mabel Evarts in 1788. Mabel's brother Ezra had earlier married Eber's niece Lorain in 1783, and another brother Curtis would later marry Eber's niece Lois in 1792. Three Evarts siblings married three Nortons across two generations — an unusually dense intermarriage between the two Guilford families.

burial.plot_notes: West Cemetery, Madison New Haven County CT. FindAGrave memorial 61367318.

parents.father_research_notes: Jonathan Evarts II (1723-1795) of Guilford CT. Per FindAGrave memorial 177556969. The 'II' suffix implies a Jonathan I — Mabel's grandfather. Father of Mabel, Ezra (X02570), and Curtis (X02571) — the three Evarts siblings who married into the Norton family across two generations.
parents.mother_research_notes: Lucretia Parmelee Evarts (1725-1795) of Guilford CT. Per FindAGrave memorial 177557121. Spelling 'Parmelee' per FG (EH spells 'Parmalee'). Both parents died 1795 — within the same year — when Mabel was 31.

narrative_blocks.header: Three Evarts siblings married three Nortons
narrative_blocks.body: Mabel Evarts married Eber Norton in 1788. Her brothers Ezra and Curtis had earlier married into the Norton family — Ezra to Eber's niece Lorain in 1783 and Curtis to Eber's niece Lois in 1792. Three Evarts siblings tied themselves to three Nortons across two generations of the same Guilford family.

narrative_blocks.header: Died two days before son's birth
narrative_blocks.body: John Norton married Ruth Dudley November 28, 1838 and died less than a year later, October 17, 1839. Their only child John William was born October 19 — two days after John's death. Ruth was widowed at 25 and gave birth to a son who would never know his father.

burial.plot_notes: West Cemetery, Madison New Haven County CT. FindAGrave.
West Cemetery, Madison New Haven County CT. FindAGrave.

narrative_blocks.header: Bidwell name traveled through three generations
narrative_blocks.body: Mary Bidwell married Anah Norton in 1791 — bringing the Hartford-Bidwell name into the Guilford-area Norton tree. Her son Stephen Bidwell Norton carried her maiden name as a middle name, and his son George Bidwell Norton did the same — three Norton generations carrying her surname forward.

burial.plot_notes: West Cemetery, Madison New Haven County CT. FindAGrave memorial 61366309.

naming_inspiration.description: Carries his mother Mary Bidwell Norton's maiden surname as middle name.

narrative_blocks.header: Lost two sons to the Civil War
narrative_blocks.body: Stephen Bidwell Norton died July 16, 1856 at age 53 — eight years before the Civil War took his son Francis M. on February 6, 1864 and six years after the war had taken his son William Erastus on December 13, 1862 at Fredericksburg. He was spared the news; Roxanna lived to see both deaths.
